# Verify that encrypted identities can be unlocked through askpass, including
# when git invokes sesam through the diff textconv path.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

# Initialize with the plain admin identity. The encrypted identity below wraps
# the same key material and should map to the same admin user once askpass
# supplies its passphrase.
exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# With askpass explicitly disabled, the encrypted identity cannot be loaded in
# this non-interactive test process.
env SESAM_ID=$ADMIN_ENCRYPTED_KEY
env SESAM_ASKPASS=$ASKPASS_HELPER
env SESAM_ASKPASS_REQUIRED=never
! exec sesam id
stderr 'failed to read passphrase'

# Force askpass and verify sesam can identify admin without terminal input.
env SESAM_ASKPASS_REQUIRED=force
exec sesam id
stdout 'admin'

# Modify and seal the bootstrap secret through the encrypted identity. This also
# verifies normal repo loading uses askpass, not just the cheap `id` path.
cp modified_readme.md README.md
exec sesam seal

# git diff invokes `sesam show` as textconv. Keep SESAM_ID pointed at the
# encrypted identity and use the askpass helper, so the git-driven path cannot
# rely on stdin prompting.
exec git diff HEAD -- .sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
stdout '# Sesam Cheatsheet'
stdout 'askpass modified readme'

-- modified_readme.md --
# askpass modified readme

This content was sealed through an askpass-unlocked identity.
